title: December 2021 Why Freight Pricing Used To Be An Art Not A Science Until Now
section_path: December 2021 Why Freight Pricing Used To Be An Art Not A Science Until Now
section_path_text: December 2021 Why Freight Pricing Used To Be An Art Not A Science Until Now
summary:
Main
By Arthur Richier

Lifting the curtain
Coffee in hand, perusing shipbroker reports is how I start most mornings. Today however, was Wednesday and that meant a weekly report from a distinguished Norwegian broker would make its way to my inbox. As I read through, one sentence in particular caught my eye: "Markets thrive on loose tongues otherwise the benchmarking process of the underlying spot market is significantly compromised".

children.title: Which is why freight pricing involves a certain je-ne-sais-quoi, akin to making it an art in which industry experience and knowledge has always been vital.
section_path: Which is why freight pricing involves a certain je-ne-sais-quoi, akin to making it an art in which industry experience and knowledge has always been vital.
children.section_path_text: Which is why freight pricing involves a certain je-ne-sais-quoi, akin to making it an art in which industry experience and knowledge has always been vital.
children.summary: In last month's edition, I wrote about The Baltic Exchange , with which we have recently partnered to bring together prices and analytics in one screen , as shown below. The Baltic Exchange price reporters, or assessors in their case, rely on the indications of shipbrokers that are in turn in close contact with charterers and shipowners across the routes they assess.

children.title: Fuel of the future: data
children.summary: Why does it require companies with a thorough understanding of the industry including relationships that take years if not decades to build? Because information is hard to come by and the markets are far from transparent.

children.title: In summary
children.summary: Whether you are fresh to most of the subjects I write about or already a veteran, there is so much still to be done when it comes to transparency in shipping markets. The level of granularity when it comes to the factors that impact freight pricing is ever-increasing, and we are racing as a company to make that data available to the market in the most transparent way.
